{"key_event":"Cartel terror and armed-group territorial expansion","reasoning":"Colombia retained functioning national institutions, elections, bureaucracy, and a strengthened formal democratic framework, but cartels, guerrillas, paramilitaries, assassinations, and the Palace of Justice crisis sharply exposed limits on coercive control and territorial authority. Civil society gained new channels through peace politics, the Unión Patriótica, and elected mayors, yet intimidation, massacres, kidnappings, and systematic violence against legal opposition reduced its effective capacity to organize and constrain power.","society_change":-0.5,"society_power":5.5,"state_change":-0.8,"state_power":6.3}
